question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger premature deletion of assessed_custom_fee records by injecting a negative Duration value into retentionProperties.getPeriod(), causing findLatestWithOffset(retentionPeriod.toNanos()) to return the most recent RecordFile and pruning all fee history including current-period fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the hiero.mirror.importer.retention.period configuration property via an exposed Spring Boot actuator or environment endpoint to set it to Duration.ZERO, causing findLatestWithOffset(0) to return the second-latest RecordFile and pruning all assessed_custom_fee and custom_fee_history records outside the intended 90-day design window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Scheduled(fixedDelayString = '#{@retentionProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()}') SpEL expression by influencing the retentionProperties bean to return a near-zero frequency, causing the prune() job to execute continuously and delete assessed_custom_fee records far faster than the design intends, effectively erasing fee audit trails?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() block to prune the assessed_custom_fee table while skipping the custom_fee_history table by manipulating the retentionProperties.exclude set to contain 'custom_fee_history', resulting in an inconsistent fee state where assessed fees exist without corresponding fee schedule history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the retentionProperties.include set to contain only 'assessed_custom_fee', causing repository.prune(endTimestamp) to delete all assessed custom fee records while leaving the transaction table intact, creating a discrepancy between transaction records and their associated fee records outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTableName(RetentionRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getTableName() method's reliance on repository.getClass().getInterfaces()[0] to produce an incorrect table name for a fee-related repository (e.g., AssessedCustomFeeRepository), causing shouldPrune() to return false and preventing legitimate fee record pruning, or conversely returning an unexpected name that bypasses the include/exclude filter and prunes fee records outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFileIterator.hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a long overflow in the expression 'current.getConsensusEnd() + batchPeriod' (line 127) by setting retentionProperties.getBatchPeriod() to a value close to Long.MAX_VALUE, resulting in a negative endTimestamp that causes findNextBetween() to return unexpected RecordFiles and prune assessed_custom_fee records outside the intended timestamp range?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFileIterator.hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user set retentionProperties.getBatchPeriod() to Duration.ZERO, causing endTimestamp to equal current.getConsensusEnd() and the condition 'endTimestamp >= max.getConsensusEnd()' to be false indefinitely, resulting in an infinite loop that continuously calls repository.prune() on assessed_custom_fee and custom_fee_history tables, deleting all fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that retentionProperties.isEnabled() is the only guard before pruning begins, and if they can flip this boolean via an exposed configuration endpoint, they can enable the retention job at will and trigger deletion of assessed_custom_fee records that are still within the intended retention window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the counters.merge(table, count, Long::sum) operation to overflow by repeatedly triggering prune() cycles with a near-zero frequency, accumulating Long.MAX_VALUE in the counter for 'assessed_custom_fee', causing arithmetic overflow that masks the true number of deleted fee records in logs and audit trails?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the negative retention period behavior (as demonstrated by findLatestWithOffset(-1) returning the latest RecordFile in tests) to set period to a negative Duration, causing the maxTimestamp to be set to the most recent consensusEnd and pruning all assessed_custom_fee records including those from the current day, outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFileIterator.hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reference equality check 'if (current == max)' (line 121) by causing the RecordFile object returned by findNextBetween() to be a different object instance with the same consensusEnd as max, preventing the iterator from terminating and causing repeated prune() calls on assessed_custom_fee records beyond the intended max timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the retentionProperties.exclude set to be empty and the include set to contain 'assessed_custom_fee' and 'custom_fee_history', causing the prune job to selectively delete only fee-related tables while preserving all other transaction data, creating an inconsistent ledger state where transactions reference non-existent fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot @ConfigurationProperties binding for 'hiero.mirror.importer.retention' to inject a crafted batchPeriod of Duration.ofNanos(1), causing the RecordFileIterator to iterate through every single RecordFile individually and call repository.prune(endTimestamp) on assessed_custom_fee for each one, resulting in excessive and unintended fee record deletion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTableName(RetentionRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Utility.toSnakeCase(Strings.CS.removeEnd(className, 'Repository')) table name derivation to produce a table name that matches an entry in the include set but corresponds to a different fee table than intended, causing the wrong fee repository's prune() to be called and deleting fee records outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a partial transaction rollback by injecting a runtime exception in one RetentionRepository.prune() call within the transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() lambda, causing some fee tables (e.g., assessed_custom_fee) to be pruned while others (e.g., custom_fee_history) are rolled back, leaving the fee data in an inconsistent state outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the initialDelay = 120_000 (2 minutes) window before the first prune() execution to inject modified retentionProperties values, ensuring that when prune() first runs it uses attacker-controlled parameters to delete assessed_custom_fee records outside the 90-day design window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the synchronized keyword on prune() only prevents concurrent execution of prune() itself, but does not prevent concurrent modification of retentionProperties, allowing a race condition where retentionProperties.getPeriod() returns a design-compliant value for the isEnabled() check but an attacker-modified value for findLatestWithOffset(), causing fee records to be pruned outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFileIterator.hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the findNextBetween(0, max.getConsensusEnd()) initialization call (line 111) which always starts from timestamp 0, causing the iterator to include the very first RecordFile in the database regardless of the retention period, and thus prune assessed_custom_fee records from the genesis of the ledger even if they should be within the retention window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the rate calculation 'long rate = elapsed > 0 ? countAfter / elapsed : 0L' (line 85) to produce a misleading log entry showing rate=0 when elapsed=0, masking the fact that a large number of assessed_custom_fee records were deleted in a single prune cycle, preventing operators from detecting fee record manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the error handling in the catch block (line 61-63) which only logs the error without re-throwing, allowing an attacker to cause a partial prune of assessed_custom_fee records and then trigger an exception to halt further pruning, leaving the fee table in a partially pruned state that is inconsistent with other tables?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user set retentionProperties.period to a very large negative Duration (e.g., Duration.ofDays(-36500)), causing retentionPeriod.toNanos() to return Long.MIN_VALUE and findLatestWithOffset() to receive a large negative offset, which per the SQL query 'consensus_end < (max) - ?1' would evaluate to consensus_end < max + |Long.MIN_VALUE|, potentially matching all records and pruning all assessed_custom_fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFileIterator.hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition 'if (endTimestamp >= max.getConsensusEnd())' (line 130) by setting batchPeriod such that endTimestamp exactly equals max.getConsensusEnd(), causing current to be set to max and the iterator to prune assessed_custom_fee records up to the most recent RecordFile, which may be within the intended retention window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the forEach loop over retentionRepositories (line 73) which has no defined ordering, causing assessed_custom_fee to be pruned before custom_fee_history in one execution and after in another, creating a window where fee records exist without their corresponding history or vice versa, enabling fee manipulation to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/env endpoint (if exposed without authentication) to override the hiero.mirror.importer.retention.period property at runtime, reducing it from the default 90 days to 1 day, causing the next scheduled prune() execution to delete assessed_custom_fee records that are 2-90 days old and outside the attacker-modified but not design-intended window?
